response:
To find the **characteristic polynomial** of a matrix $ A $, we compute the determinant of $ A - \lambda I $, where $ I $ is the identity matrix of the same size as $ A $.

Given matrix:

$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
-4 & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14
\end{pmatrix}
$$

We compute $ A - \lambda I $:

$$
A - \lambda I = \begin{pmatrix}
-4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{pmatrix}
$$

Now compute the determinant:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = \begin{vmatrix}
-4 - \lambda & -13 & 10 \\
-9 & 10 - \lambda & 4 \\
12 & -5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

We will expand this determinant using cofactor expansion or row operations. Let's expand along the first row:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = (-4 - \lambda) \cdot \begin{vmatrix}
10 - \lambda & 4 \\
-5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
- (-13) \cdot \begin{vmatrix}
-9 & 4 \\
12 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
+ 10 \cdot \begin{vmatrix}
-9 & 10 - \lambda \\
12 & -5
\end{vmatrix}
$$

---

1. $ \begin{vmatrix}
10 - \lambda & 4 \\
-5 & -14 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix} = (10 - \lambda)(-14 - \lambda) - (4)(-5) $  
$$
= -140 - 10\lambda + 14\lambda + \lambda^2 + 20 = \lambda^2 + 4\lambda - 120
$$
